author: barnabasbusa
content: what was the conclusion of this? Are we ok to have the EL as an optional flag now?

author: barnabasbusa
content: I think requiring the EL post optional proofs is just silly. Large node operators will not rely on proofs, small operators can afford to miss out on a slot here and there.

author: jih2nn
content: I think it's up to design decision. I don't have any strong opinions on either way.

author: jih2nn
content: Note that optional proofs targets some time between Glamsterdam and Hegotá. Making the execution engine necessary wouldn't make small operators miss their slots.

author: barnabasbusa
content: currently the cl spec says that its a must have

author: barnabasbusa
content: so could we drop that definition and make it optional?

author: barnabasbusa
content: e.g I wanna be able to run a CL without defining the EL engine api point and also make the jwt secret an optional flag going forward

author: jih2nn
content: I would say the designs are not set in stone yet but I'd defer answering this optionality to zkEVM team.

author: taulepton_
content: So the main reason that we had concluded that we should ship with EL required for optional proofs is because in the failure case where execution proofs become unavailable (for whatever reason) the validator has no recovery path. If they have no EL then they have no EL state and as such can not "fall back" to using EL blocks for validation.

author: taulepton_
content: Imo I think supporting no EL for optional proofs is a nice to have but should not be a blocker. I think we need to build strong confidence that the system can run reliably without an EL and failure cases are unlikely / uncommon before we bake it into the protocol for optional proofs. For mandatory proofs no EL is a hard requirement.

author: barnabasbusa
content: I would still phrase it that having engine api “highly recommend” but not must have.

author: taulepton_
content: I think this is reasonable. I would also cc <@427491045308235776> for input on this topic.
